title: KSMS School ERP
summary: Full school management ERP with 20+ modules for 1,500+ students — covering admissions, attendance, exams, fees, and parent communication.
description: A production ERP system for a school with 1,500+ active students. The platform has 20+ functional modules: student admissions and profiles, class and section management, daily attendance tracking, exam scheduling and result generation, fee collection with payment history, timetable management, transport tracking, and internal notices. Built with role-based access control for 4 user types — admin, teacher, student, and parent — each with tailored dashboards and scoped permissions. All data is centralized in MongoDB. The system replaced entirely manual processes (paper registers, spreadsheets) and is self-hosted on a VPS for data privacy and uptime control.
results: Shipped 20+ modules covering the full school workflow from admissions to result generation
Implemented RBAC for 4 user roles: admin, teacher, student, and parent
Automated fee collection, attendance tracking, and exam result generation
Replaced paper-based processes for 1,500+ students across the institution
Deployed and maintained on VPS with full data ownership and privacy control

title: Office Management System
summary: Internal office management platform with 15+ modules — HR, payroll, attendance, leave, project tracking, invoicing, and role-based access control.
description: A full-featured office management system built as an internal platform for business operations. Covers 15+ modules: HR management, payroll processing, daily attendance, leave management, project and task tracking, invoicing, expense management, and role-based access control. Each employee role (admin, HR, manager, employee) gets a scoped dashboard with only the data and actions relevant to them. The system was built to eliminate spreadsheet-based operations and consolidate business workflows into one platform. Private deployment — available on request for walkthrough.
results: Built 15+ modules covering HR, payroll, attendance, leave, and project tracking
Implemented role-based access for admin, HR, manager, and employee roles
Automated payroll calculation and invoice generation workflows
Centralized business operations previously managed across multiple spreadsheets
Designed a modular architecture allowing new modules to be added independently

title: AI Mock Interview Platform
summary: AI-powered interview practice platform — users speak answers, get real-time Gemini API feedback, and track performance across custom interview sessions.
description: A developer side-project that combines speech-to-text input with Gemini API analysis to simulate real interview sessions. Users select a topic or role, answer questions verbally, and receive structured AI feedback on their response quality, relevance, and clarity. Built with Next.js and Node.js for the full-stack layer, the Web Speech API for voice capture, and Google's Gemini API for answer evaluation. The project was an exercise in working with LLM APIs, managing real-time audio input, and building a clean feedback UX.
results: Integrated Gemini API to evaluate spoken interview answers with structured feedback
Built speech-to-text pipeline using the Web Speech API for real-time voice capture
Designed session-based interview flow with topic selection and score tracking
Gained hands-on experience with LLM API integration and prompt engineering

title: Expertsofdeals
summary: B2B marketplace connecting 4,000+ buyers and 2,000+ suppliers. Built and maintained 15+ REST APIs and a 20+ module admin dashboard.
description: Expertsofdeals is a live B2B marketplace platform at my current role. I own the backend API layer — 15+ REST endpoints built with Node.js and Express serving real marketplace operations across supplier onboarding, buyer inquiries, product listings, and order flows. On the frontend, I built the admin dashboard with React, Next.js, and Redux — covering 20+ operational modules including analytics, listing management, user verification, and inquiry tracking. The platform runs on a self-managed Linux VPS via Dokploy with 99%+ uptime. It currently serves 4,000+ registered buyers and 2,000+ suppliers across multiple product categories.
results: Built and maintained 15+ REST APIs serving 4,000+ buyers and 2,000+ suppliers
Developed a 20+ module admin dashboard for analytics and marketplace operations
Maintained 99%+ uptime on self-managed Linux VPS using Dokploy
Implemented supplier verification flow and buyer inquiry system end-to-end
Optimized public-facing pages with Next.js SSR for SEO and fast load times

title: Travelliq
summary: A travel destination website where users can explore destinations and book travel packages — built with a MERN backend and Next.js frontend.
description: Travelliq is a travel destination platform that lets users browse curated travel destinations and book packages directly through the site. The frontend is built with Next.js for fast, SEO-optimized destination pages. The backend is a MERN stack — Node.js and Express handle the API layer, MongoDB stores destinations, packages, and booking data, and React powers the admin-side management. Users can explore destinations, view package details and pricing, and complete bookings through a streamlined flow. The platform is designed to scale with new destinations and package types without structural changes.
results: Built destination browsing and package listing pages with Next.js SSR for SEO performance
Developed REST APIs for destinations, packages, and booking management with Node.js and Express
Implemented end-to-end booking flow with package selection, user details, and confirmation
Stored and managed destinations, packages, and bookings in MongoDB
Designed a scalable data model allowing new destinations and packages to be added independently

title: Traders Mart
summary: B2C e-commerce platform with decoupled architecture — separate storefront, API server, and admin panel, each independently deployable.
description: A B2C e-commerce platform built with a clean three-layer architecture: a Next.js storefront for the public shopping experience, a Node.js/Express API server handling all business logic, and a separate React admin panel for product, order, and inventory management. Each layer is independently deployable and scalable. The platform includes product catalog, cart, checkout, order processing, inventory management, and JWT-authenticated user accounts. Hosted on Cloudflare + VPS for performance and availability.
results: Architected a decoupled three-layer system: storefront, API server, and admin panel
Built REST APIs for catalog, cart, checkout, orders, and inventory
Implemented JWT authentication and session management for user accounts
Deployed across Cloudflare and VPS for performance, reliability, and availability
